summary: Read-only verification of how the scheduler/adjustment/USER-request flows handle semesters. Classified each flow's mode (A. ACTIVE_SEMESTER_ONLY, B. GLOBAL_SELECTOR, C. PAGE_SELECTOR, D. API_ONLY, E. CROSS_SEMESTER_RISK). No schema/DB/business-logic changes.

crossSemesterRiskDetails.USER_submit_approve: LOW (prevented by sourceSlot.semesterId === resolved.id check in service)
crossSemesterRiskDetails.USER_dry_run_recommend: LOW (uses active semester; source slot check on submit/approve prevents writes)
crossSemesterRiskDetails.USER_mine_listing: HIGH (returns ALL semesters for the user; no page-level filter)
crossSemesterRiskDetails.ADMIN_list_page: MEDIUM (returns ALL semesters; page doesn't expose ?semesterId= filter)
crossSemesterRiskDetails.Scheduler: LOW (uses unique active semester; apply/rollback derive from run record)

recommendedNextStage: K28-D-SEMESTER-SWITCHING-UX-IMPLEMENTATION (if UX requires per-page semester selector) OR K28-B-USER-ADJUSTMENT-APPROVAL-FLOW-MANUAL-TRIAL (if current behavior is acceptable)
